Elateriopsis pittieri belongs to the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard-apple family. This family consists of over 160 genera and 2,200 species of plants. Elateriopsis pittieri is further classified under the genus Elateriopsis, which is a small genus within the Annonaceae family. The classification of Elateriopsis pittieri is part of ongoing research in the field of botany and taxonomy.

intro: Elateriopsis pittieri, also known as Pitted Schisandra or Schisandra pittieri, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, making it useful for supporting cardiovascular health, improving cognitive function, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Elateriopsis pittieri include lignans, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Elateriopsis pittieri is often used to make herbal preparations such as capsules, teas, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
